meta.session_protocol: single sitting, ~75 min, batched 6 items per turn (final batch 3); no in-session feedback; items mixed across clusters round-robin
meta.scoring_rubric: target-tracking (did the L1 trap targeted by the item get handled correctly), with notes on collateral errors

scoring_bands.correct: target L1 trap handled correctly; collateral errors permitted but flagged
scoring_bands.partial: target trap partially handled or response shows competence with deficits on target
scoring_bands.incorrect: target trap failed; L1 calque present or response avoided the structure
scoring_bands.avoidance: response sidestepped the structure being tested via a valid paraphrase that bypassed the diagnostic target

responses.response: The
responses.notes: post-modified definite handled

responses.response: on
responses.notes: depends on — collocation correct

responses.response: postponing
responses.notes: suggest + gerund correct

responses.response: have lived
responses.notes: present perfect for state since past

responses.response: introduced
responses.notes: type 2 if-clause past simple

responses.response: I'd like to understand what is the real bottleneck on the procurement side before escalating this.
responses.notes: did NOT fix the embedded-question inversion ('what is the real bottleneck'); changed unrelated 'before I escalate' → 'before escalating' instead
generic bare plural correct

responses.response: for
responses.notes: wait for

responses.response: training
responses.notes: avoid + gerund

responses.response: He said he was tired and (he) needed a break.
responses.notes: sequence-of-tenses backshift recognised

responses.response: If I checked the weather forecast, I would not go out on that ride.
responses.notes: picked type 2 for a counterfactual past — should have been type 3 ('had checked, would not have gone'). Russian-single-conditional collapse

responses.response: I always start my day with a quick review of overnight comments.
responses.notes: frequency adverb placement correct

responses.response: a
responses.notes: Ionin-FH [+def −spec] cell — should have picked 'the'; chose the L1-trap calque 'a'

responses.response: of
responses.notes: consist of

responses.response: working
responses.notes: enjoy + gerund

responses.response: When I got to the cafe, the rest of the group had been already left without me.
responses.notes: recognised perfect was needed but produced ungrammatical 'had been already left' (perfect-passive form on intransitive verb). Direction right, form wrong

responses.response: If the CFO approve the budget tomorrow, we could start hiring next week.
responses.notes: correctly removed 'would' from if-clause (L1 trap caught) but used bare form 'approve' instead of past simple 'approved'

responses.response: do you
responses.notes: do-support in present-simple question

responses.response: Success in our line of work depends on relationships, not on credentials alone.
responses.notes: dropped generic-abstract 'the'

responses.response: We arrived to the office an hour late because the bridge was closed for a maintenance.
responses.notes: did NOT fix 'arrive to' L1 trap (target); introduced new error 'for a maintenance' (mass noun + indef article hyper-correction)

responses.response: I would recommend to wait till Q3 before re-opening the question with the regulator.
responses.notes: did NOT fix 'recommend to wait' — target L1 trap untouched

responses.response: We are already working on the contract for 8 months, but it's still far from completion.
responses.notes: used present continuous instead of present perfect continuous; canonical L1-ru tense trap surfaced

responses.response: if I learned Arabic in the first year, it would be much easier for me to address house problems.
responses.notes: got result-clause structure ('would be ... now'); failed if-clause — should be 'had learnt' past perfect for mixed conditional. Russian-single-conditional collapse; minor lexical calque 'address house problems' from 'бытовые вопросы'

responses.response: none in the team knows for sure why they have decreased the price in the last moment.
responses.notes: embedded-question word order correct (no inversion — good); collateral errors: 'none in the team' (should be 'nobody on the team'), present perfect for specific past, 'in the last moment' (should be 'at the last moment/minute')

responses.response: The house we have already lived in for three years is owned by a British expat.
responses.notes: definite post-modifier handled correctly + 'a British expat' indefinite correct; DROPPED the second clause entirely ('который сдаёт его через агента'). Truncation worth flagging — could indicate complexity-driven simplification

responses.response: it's unlikely that we succeed in this tender unless we improve the Commercial part.
responses.notes: 'succeed in' collocation correct; capitalisation 'Commercial' is typing artefact

responses.response: despite of delays, the team managed to close the quarter with major adjustments.
responses.notes: 'manage to + inf' correct (the target). Two collateral errors: 'despite of' (should be 'despite' or 'in spite of'); POLARITY FLIP — 'with major adjustments' instead of 'without significant adjustments'. Semantic miscarriage worth flagging

responses.response: on yesterday's meeting Claude said that this approach works better that the previous one we tried last week.
responses.notes: Three target patterns missed in one sentence: (1) NO backshift on 'works' (target trap surfaced); (2) than/that confusion 'better that the previous'; (3) NO past perfect on 'tried'. Plus collateral 'on yesterday's meeting' (preposition)

responses.response: if we have agreed to their terms in January, the contract will have already been active.
responses.notes: wrong tense across both clauses: present perfect in if-clause (should be past perfect 'had agreed'), 'will have already been' (type 1 future perfect) instead of 'would already be / would already have been' (mixed or type 3). Russian-single-conditional collapse with type 1 substitution

responses.response: on weekends I usually ride before sunrise to avoid the heat
responses.notes: frequency adverb placement + clean structure

responses.response: last week I was contacted by a recruiter with an interesting proposal from a Middle Eastern oil and gas company.
responses.notes: all three indefinite NPs handled correctly under Ionin-FH [−def +spec]; passive voice substitution natural

responses.response: I excused myself to a neighbor for the noise we made on the weekend.
responses.notes: lexical substitution 'excused myself' for 'apologised' — bypasses the apologise + to/for preposition test; 'excuse oneself to' isn't natural for 'apologise to' in this register

responses.response: I'm trying to practice entering into turns on high speed on Fridays.
responses.notes: practice + gerund (target) correct; collateral 'on high speed' should be 'at high speed' (preposition L1 trap, separate from cluster target)

responses.response: to the moment, when I start, other have been already covered the first ten kilometers.
responses.notes: Multiple errors: 'to the moment' L1 calque (should be 'by the time'); present 'start' instead of past 'got/arrived'; 'other' missing article+plural ('the others'); 'have been already covered' malformed past perfect (mixes passive 'have been' with active form). Strong tense_aspect signal

responses.response: if there is a strong wind from the desert, I move my training to the evening. If tomorrow is the same weather, I will ride out in the early morning.
responses.notes: zero conditional + type 1 both handled cleanly. Awkward word order 'if tomorrow is the same weather' but no 'will' in if-clause, no type-collapse

responses.response: please send me this report in pdf and attach it in the shared chat.
responses.notes: phrasal verb separability correct ('attach it in' — pronoun between V and prep); 'attach in' lexically off (should be 'drop/post in') but doesn't fail the word-order target

responses.response: Sleep and recovery is more important for progress than a training load.
responses.notes: generic-mass articles (Sleep, recovery, progress) all correctly zero — main test passed. Collateral: 'is' should be 'are' (compound subject agreement); 'a training load' wrong article (mass noun + indef article)

responses.response: when I ride for long distances, I usually listen to economics podcasts.
responses.notes: 'listen to' correct (target); collateral 'ride for long distances' — 'for' is L1 slip (should be 'do long rides' or 'ride long distances')

responses.response: on the way to work I stopped to reread their latest letter one more time.
responses.notes: 'stopped to reread' — purpose reading correct, NOT cessation. Cluster 6 'meaning-change' verbs handled well

responses.response: the procurement department has just been approved our order, but I haven't yet seen the document.
responses.notes: second clause 'haven't yet seen' correct present perfect (the test target). First clause 'has just been approved our order' — passive/active blend (should be active 'has just approved'). Mid-attempt structural confusion

responses.response: many
responses.notes: many with count noun under negation

responses.response: on
responses.notes: focus on + gerund

responses.response: They must have lost network coverage — the whole industrial area was down at three.
responses.notes: modal + perfect inf for past speculation

responses.response: is
responses.notes: collective noun singular (accepted variant)

responses.response: it
responses.notes: expletive it for weather

responses.response: C and D (flagged as identical); on second look picked D
responses.notes: First-pass: could not distinguish 'almost so competitive as' (C, archaic/L1-calque) from 'almost as competitive as' (D, modern correct). On second look identified D. Sub-signal: structural distinction perceptible under attention but not on first read

responses.response: i usually arrive to the office at 8am, then go for a short walk and drink coffee. right now I'm busy with new KPIs for the Finance team. I'm very afraid of an upcoming presentation of the produced KPIs to our GCFO.
responses.notes: Article handling generally clean: zero on 'KPIs' (generic plural) and 'coffee' (mass); 'the Finance team' definite correct. Did NOT hit indefinite-generic-class feature explicitly. REPEATED L1 TRAP: 'arrive to the office' (same trap as item 20, item 71 — surfaced 3x total). Lexical 'very afraid of upcoming presentation' off-register

responses.response: the team had agreed with me on the priorities, but we are still discussing the timeline.
responses.notes: 'agree with [person] on [matter]' — both prepositions correct (target). Collateral: 'had agreed' past perfect without anchor for it (probably no past-time-frame trigger); not a cluster target error

responses.response: our neighbor helped us to assemble a bike on the weekend.
responses.notes: help + to-inf accepted (bare-inf 'helped us assemble' is more natural in BrE but 'to' form is valid); article 'a bike' defensible as first-mention indefinite

responses.response: We have very little time left if we want to ride before the heat builds up.
responses.notes: 'little' with mass noun 'time' — count/mass distinction correct

responses.response: in
responses.notes: interested in + gerund

responses.response: should have renewed
responses.notes: should + perfect inf for past regret/advice

responses.response: Could you share any information you have on how other teams are using the assistant for similar workflows?
responses.notes: removed plural -s from uncountable 'information'

responses.response: there
responses.notes: expletive there with locative inversion

responses.response: The new wheelset feels lighter on climbs, and more responsive in the corners.
responses.notes: removed 'more' from 'more lighter' (double comparative fixed); kept 'more responsive' (correct analytic comparative)

responses.response: I don't think there is any good argument for changing the approach right now.
responses.notes: swapped 'some' → 'any' under negation; singularised 'arguments' → 'argument' (valid simplification)

responses.response: I look forward to hearing back from your team on the revised commercial terms.
responses.notes: 'to hear' → 'to hearing' — preposition + gerund classic trap caught

responses.response: he must have gone home - his car is not in the parking lot.
responses.notes: must + perfect inf for past speculation

responses.response: Everyone in the Saturday group are supposed to bring their own spare tube.
responses.notes: picked plural 'are' with indefinite-pronoun subject 'everyone' — canonical L1-ru trap (Russian 'все' is plural). The target distractor

responses.response: This is an interesting question, but I don't think we have the bandwidth to take it on this quarter.
responses.notes: added missing 'an' (L1 trap fixed); 'This' vs 'That' choice — accepted either

responses.response: their proposal is not as good as we want it to be, but it's not that bad to stop the negotiations.
responses.notes: got 'not as good as' (target); attempted result-clause 'not that bad to stop' — should be 'not bad enough to walk away' (result construction with 'enough'). Approximation, not full structure

responses.response: There are too much people in the project and too little time to coordinate everything.
responses.notes: 'too little time' correct (mass); 'too much people' INCORRECT (people = count → many). Asymmetric pattern — mass dimension solid, count dimension fails under production

responses.response: We managed to cut the time of contract approval in two without affecting quality.
responses.notes: 'without + gerund' (cluster target — alternative form valid); 'manage to + inf' instead of 'succeed in + gerund' — avoided primary target via valid paraphrase. Counted correct as the prep+gerund slot was filled by 'without affecting'

responses.response: due to the new club rules, we have to sign in for group ride one day in advance.
responses.notes: 'have to' (external obligation — target). Collateral: 'sign in for' should be 'sign up for' (phrasal verb); 'group ride' missing article/plural

responses.response: we need more recent data and best practices on regulator issues, before we start approaching a regulator.
responses.notes: SUBSTITUTED 'best practices' for 'advice' — bypassed the advice/uncountable test. Strong avoidance signature. Plus 'a regulator' should be 'the regulator' (specific known entity)

responses.response: today's morning is cool and fresh - it's ideal weather for a long training session.
responses.notes: second clause 'it's ideal weather' — used expletive it correctly. First clause 'today's morning is cool' — L1 calque 'today's morning' (should be 'this morning'); also PRESENT tense for a past state ('было прохладно' → was, not is). Tense + deixis errors

responses.response: The earlier you start driving, the lower is the traffic along the corniche.
responses.notes: 'the + comparative, the + comparative' correlative construction handled. Copula inversion 'is the traffic' acceptable in this construction

responses.response: We have got several good restaurants in the neighborhood, but a very few cafe with third wave coffee.
responses.notes: 'several' substitutes 'a few' (lexically valid). 'a very few cafe' MALFORMED — 'a few' and 'very few' have opposite polarity; cannot be combined; 'cafe' should be 'cafes'. Polarity awareness incomplete

responses.response: Instead of doing the long ride in this heat, I made an internal training yesterday on a trainer.
responses.notes: 'Instead of doing' (prep + gerund — target). Collateral: 'the long ride' (should be 'a long ride' / 'long rides'); 'made an internal training' — make/do calque (should be 'did an interval session'); 'internal' = typo for 'interval'

responses.response: You don't need to write everything in one sentence - you can split it in several parts.
responses.notes: 'don't need to' for absence of obligation (target — correctly NOT 'mustn't'); collateral lexical 'sentence' (stem said 'сообщении'=message); 'in several parts' should be 'into several parts'

responses.response: There are many families in our neighborhood, which arrived to Bahrain for work and stayed because of schools.
responses.notes: 'there are + plural' agreement correct (target). REPEATED L1 TRAP: 'arrived to Bahrain' (3rd surface — same as items 20 and 47). Also 'which' should be 'who' (relative pronoun for people)

responses.response: It was the finance department, not the board, that blocked the initiative in the last quarter.
responses.notes: it-cleft construction handled cleanly — strong B2/C1 structure

responses.response: The new version is much faster for a joint work, but it is still twice as expensive.
responses.notes: 'much faster' (intensifier with comparative — target); 'twice as expensive' (multiplicative — target). Collateral: 'for a joint work' (mass noun + article — should be 'for joint work' or 'for collaboration')

responses.response: I don't see any reason to delay the decision for one more quarter.
responses.notes: 'any' under negation (target); collateral 'for one more quarter' could be 'by another quarter' but 'for' is defensible

responses.response: The corporate IT department insists that we route all traffic via their VPN.
responses.notes: 'insists that + subjunctive' — valid paraphrase that avoided the L1 trap 'insists to do'; preposition + gerund target sidestepped but cluster intent met via subjunctive form
